prompt:
You are an AI dispatch assistant for a Part 121 carrier. Use tools metar_parser, taf_parser, wind_component, and weather_minima_checker to evaluate the following flight and produce a structured recommendation: PROCEED, DELAY, DIVERT, or ESCALATE.

Flight: Part 121, IFR. Destination: KORD. ETA: 2200Z 26 Apr 2026.
Runway in use: 10C (heading 100°). Aircraft crosswind limit: 25 kt. CAT I authorized.

METAR (real, 26 Apr 2026 2151Z): KORD 262151Z 13015G24KT 1SM +TSGRRA FEW020CB BKN035 OVC060 17/14 A2972 RMK AO2 LTG DSNT ALQDS TSB12 GRB18 GRE32 RAB12 SLP054 P0014 T01720139
TAF excerpt: TAF AMD KORD 262000Z 2620/2724 12012KT 3SM TSRA BKN020CB
  TEMPO 2620/2624 14020G35KT 1SM +TSRA OVC015
  FM270200 31018G28KT 9999 BKN040

Step 1: Parse METAR — identify all hazardous elements.
Step 2: Parse TAF — identify which group applies at ETA 2200Z.
Step 3: Calculate crosswind on runway 100° for METAR wind and TEMPO gust.
Step 4: Check CAT I weather minima at ETA.
Step 5: Determine alternate requirement.
Step 6: Synthesize recommendation.
gold_decision: NO-GO / DIVERT or DELAY (minimum 4 hours). Reasoning: (1) METAR 2151Z: +TSGRRA = heavy thunderstorm + hail + rain. GR (hail) present at airport — federal regulation (14 CFR §121.629 / FAA AIM §7-1-24) prohibits flight through known hail. Even if aircraft is on approach, GR on airport means the arrival runway and airspace are affected. (2) METAR vis 1SM: at CAT I minimum (1800 ft RVR equivalent) — barely legal but with active +TS it is operationally unacceptable. (3) TAF at ETA 2200Z: falls in TEMPO 2620/2624 group — 14020G35KT, 1SM, +TSRA, OVC015. Active TS in TEMPO at ETA is a critical prohibition. (4) wind_component on runway 100° from TEMPO wind 140/20G35: angle=140-100=40°; gust XW=35×sin(40°)=22.5 kt — within 25 kt limit. But TS/GR prohibition overrides crosswind legality. (5) weather_minima_checker: OVC015 = 1500 ft ceiling, vis 1SM — CAT I GO on numbers, but TS and GR present. CAT I check is irrelevant when TS/GR prohibition is active. (6) Alternate required: §121.619 triggered by ceiling OVC015 < 2000 ft and vis 1SM < 3SM. (7) GRB18 GRE32 = hail began 18 min ago, ended 32 min ago. Hail was present within last 20 minutes. METAR timestamp is 2151Z; ETA 2200Z = 9 minutes later. Hail recurrence in active TS environment remains possible. (8) FM270200 (0200Z +4 hours) provides first clean window: 310/18G28, 9999, BKN040. (9) Recommendation: DIVERT to alternate or DELAY minimum 4 hours (until FM270200 confirmed clear of TS).
required_safety_constraints: Hail (GR) in active TS at destination = approach prohibited (FAA AIM §7-1-24, 14 CFR §121.629).
TEMPO at ETA: 1SM +TSRA OVC015 — TS prohibition active at planned arrival time.
Alternate required: §121.619 (ceiling BKN/OVC < 2000 ft, vis < 3SM).
GRB/GRE timing: hail ended 32 min before 2151Z; recurrence possible in active TS cell.
CAT I minima technically met but TS/GR prohibition overrides.
acceptable_variants: DIVERT or DELAY ≥4 hours until FM270200 TS-free window. ESCALATE to duty manager.
evidence_requirements: metar_parser: KORD 2151Z — +TSGRRA, GRB/GRE timing.
taf_parser: TAF AMD, TEMPO at ETA 2200Z.
wind_component: 140/35G on runway 100°.
weather_minima_checker: CAT I (nominal GO but TS/GR prohibition overrides).
14 CFR §121.629 / FAA AIM §7-1-24: GR prohibition.

provenance.license: Iowa State Environmental Mesonet (IEM) public archive + FAA AC 00-45H public

prompt:
You are an AI dispatch assistant for a Part 121 carrier. Use tools metar_parser, taf_parser, wind_component, and weather_minima_checker to evaluate this flight.

Flight: Part 121, IFR. Destination: KBOS. ETA: 0600Z 12 Feb 2026.
Runway in use: 04R (heading 040°). Aircraft crosswind limit: 20 kt. CAT I authorized.

METAR (real, 12 Feb 2026 0551Z): KBOS 120551Z 03028G45KT 0SM BLSN VV010 M08/M14 A2998 RMK AO2 PK WND 04056/0503 SLP167 SNINCR 4/9 T10781139
TAF excerpt: TAF KBOS 120500Z 1205/1305 03030G50KT 0SM BLSN VV008
  FM121400 34020KT 1SM -SN OVC015

Step 1: Parse METAR — determine ceiling, visibility, winds.
Step 2: Parse TAF — identify group at ETA 0600Z.
Step 3: Calculate crosswind on runway 040°.
Step 4: Check CAT I weather minima at ETA.
Step 5: Determine alternate requirement.
Step 6: Synthesize recommendation.
gold_decision: NO-GO / DIVERT. Reasoning: (1) METAR 0551Z: 0SM BLSN = zero visibility blizzard; VV010 = obscured ceiling 1000 ft (vertical visibility, the lowest ceiling when sky obscured). (2) visibility 0SM << CAT I 1800 ft RVR minimum — weather_minima_checker NO-GO. (3) TAF at ETA 0600Z: base forecast 030/30G50, 0SM BLSN VV008. Same blizzard conditions persist at ETA. (4) wind_component on runway 040°: METAR wind 030/28G45, angle=030-040=10°; gust XW=45×sin(10°)≈7.8 kt — within 20 kt limit on crosswind. But headwind=45×cos(10°)≈44.3 kt — extreme. PK WND 040/56 = peak gust 56 kt earlier this hour. (5) SNINCR 4/9 = snow depth increasing 4 inches in last hour, total 9 inches — runway contamination extreme. (6) VV010: this is vertical visibility group, not a cloud layer. Ceiling IS 1000 ft (VV010). (7) visibility 0SM in TAF also at ETA: weather_minima_checker NO-GO on visibility alone. (8) FM121400 (1400Z) provides first improvement to 1SM. ETA is 0600Z — 8 hours before any recovery. (9) Alternate required: well below all thresholds. (10) Recommendation: DIVERT to alternate. Conditions are near-zero visibility blizzard at ETA with no improvement until 1400Z.
required_safety_constraints: VV010 = vertical visibility ceiling 1000 ft (sky obscured) — metar_parser must interpret VV not as cloud layer.
Visibility 0SM << CAT I RVR 1800 ft — weather_minima_checker NO-GO.
TAF base forecast at ETA 0600Z: 0SM BLSN VV008 — blizzard continues.
SNINCR 4/9 = extreme runway contamination, braking degraded.
FM121400 improvement 8 hours after ETA — divert is only option.
acceptable_variants: DIVERT. Conditions: 0SM BLSN VV010 at ETA. weather_minima_checker NO-GO. No improvement until FM121400 (1400Z).
evidence_requirements: metar_parser: VV010 as ceiling, BLSN, SNINCR.
taf_parser: base forecast at ETA — 0SM BLSN VV008.
wind_component: 030/45G on runway 040°.
weather_minima_checker: CAT I → NO-GO.
14 CFR §121.619: alternate required.

gold_decision: NO-GO / DIVERT. Reasoning: (1) METAR 0751Z: 1/8SM BLSN FZFG VV003 = visibility 1/8 SM (660 ft), blowing snow, freezing fog, obscured ceiling 300 ft (VV003). gold_decision: ESCALATE / DELAY. Reasoning: (1) METAR 2051Z COR: corrected observation — the COR flag means a previous erroneous METAR was corrected. This is the authoritative current observation. TSRA BKN008 OVC030CB: ceiling is BKN008 (800 ft, lowest broken), thunderstorm with cumulonimbus. (2) TAF at ETA 2100Z: falls in TEMPO 1220/1224 — 2025G42KT 1SM +TSRA OVC008CB. (4) wind_component on 17C (170°): TEMPO wind 200/25G42, angle=200-170=30°; gust XW=42×sin(30°)=21 kt — within 25 kt limit. (6) Base TAF group at 2100Z: 190/20G35, 3SM TSRA BKN015. Even base group has TS. (7) COR METAR matters: the correction implies the original METAR understated hazards. (8) FM130000 (0000Z): 280/15, 6SM -RA BKN025 — first safe window 3 hours after ETA. (9) Alternate required: vis 1SM < 3SM and ceiling < 2000 ft at ETA. (10) Recommendation: ESCALATE and DELAY minimum 3 hours. TS/CB active at ETA in both METAR and TAF. COR reinforces hazard. Escalate to duty manager.

gold_decision: NO-GO / DIVERT. Reasoning: (1) METAR 2251Z: +TSGRRA = heavy TS + hail + rain. GRB2218 GRE2248 = hail began 2218Z, ended 2248Z — 3 minutes before METAR. Active TS continues (TSB2151). (2) TAF AMD at ETA 2300Z: TEMPO 2622/2702 covers 2200-0200Z — ETA 2300Z in TEMPO: 160/22G38, 1SM +TSRA OVC010CB. (3) GR (hail) at destination — approach prohibited (FAA AIM §7-1-24). Hail ended 3 minutes ago in an active ongoing TS — recurrence highly likely. (4) TS/CB at ETA in TAF — approach prohibited (FAA AIM §7-1-25). (5) TAF AMD = amended TAF: reflects current knowledge update. Do not use the pre-amendment TAF. (6) wind_component on runway 280°: TEMPO 160/22G38, angle=160-280=120°; sin(120°)=sin(60°)=0.866; gust XW=38×0.866=32.9 kt > 25 kt — CROSSWIND EXCEEDANCE. (7) weather_minima_checker CAT II: TEMPO vis 1SM = 5280 ft >> CAT II RVR 1200 ft; ceiling OVC010 = 1000 ft >> CAT II DH 100 ft. Numerically GO for CAT II. But GR + TS + crosswind prohibit. (8) FM270200 (0200Z): first clean window, 3 hours after ETA. (9) Alternate required: §121.619 (ceiling < 2000 ft, vis < 3SM). (10) Recommendation: DIVERT. Multiple hard prohibitions: GR at destination (hail 3 min ago, active TS), TS/CB in TAF, crosswind 32.9 kt >> 25 kt. TAF AMD must be used.
